channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Belarus strongman wins a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 22 reported killed in Lebanon before agreement to extend deadline for Israeli forces to withdraw
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Sunday opened fire on protesters demanding their withdrawal in line with a ceasefire agreement, killing at least 22 and injuring 124, Lebanese health officials reported.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro on Sunday ordered an increase of import tariffs on goods from the United States in retaliation to President Trump's tariffs and sanctions. Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Deal reached to release Israeli hostage and allow Palestinians into north Gaza
item.description: Mediator Qatar announced early Monday that an agreement has been reached to release an Israeli civilian hostage and allow Palestinians to return to northern Gaza, easing the first major crisis of the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Moreno says he will introduce legislation to sanction Colombia
item.description: Sen. Bernie Moreno (R-Ohio) said Sunday he will introduce legislation to sanction Colombia this week after the country’s president reportedly denied entry to two planes carrying deported migrants from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: The authorship of The Associated Press’s renowned “napalm girl” photograph is being called into question in the new documentary “The Stringer,” which premiered at the Sundance Film Festival in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Holocaust memorial chair: Musk's call for Germany to 'move beyond' Nazi guilt is dangerous
item.description: The chair of Yad Vashem, Israel’s official Holocaust memorial, said Sunday tech billionaire Elon Musk’s call for Germans to “move beyond” the crimes of Nazi Germany is dangerous for the country’s democratic future.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: Colombia will send its presidential plane to Honduras to pick up Colombians after the country refused to accept migrant deportation flights from the United States, causing President Trump to enact tariffs and other retaliatory measures on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, The Hill

item.title: About 70 people killed in attack on hospital in Sudan's Darfur region, WHO chief says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Around 70 people were killed in an attack on the only functional hospital in the besieged city of El Fasher in Sudan, the chief of the World Health Organization said Sunday, part of a series of attacks coming as the African nation's civil war escalated in recent da
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus frees American woman from detention as strongman leader looks to extend 30-year rule
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Belarus has “unilaterally” freed an American woman from detention.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia’s president says he will deny entry of US deportation flights
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro said he will deny entry to the United States's deportation flights as President Trump's immigration plan begins. “The US cannot treat Colombian migrants as criminals,” Petro posted Sunday to the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US, Colombia clash over deportations and raise tariffs in show of Trump's pledge to limit migration
item.description: The United States and Colombia, long close partners in anti-narcotics efforts, are clashing over the deportation of migrants and imposed tariffs on each other’s goods in a show of what countries could face if they intervene in the Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on the thousands disappeared in Colombia, Peru and Paraguay
item.description: Thousands of people have disappeared in Latin America during decades-long conflicts. Many have never been found, presumed to be the victims of dictatorships, insurgencies or organized crime.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukrainian human rights lawyer, Nobel Prize winner urges Trump to show strength and end the war
item.description: Ukrainian human rights attorney and Nobel Peace Prize winner Oleksandra Matviichuk urges President Trump to show strength as he tries to bring the Russia-Ukraine war to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia's disappeared: As a coalition, former enemies now search for loved ones and dream of peace
item.description: CALI, Colombia (AP) — From time to time, Gustavo Arbeláez faces relatives whose losses were caused by the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC), the powerful guerrilla group he was part of during Colombia’s five-decade armed conflict.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas releases 4 female hostages as part of Israel ceasefire deal
item.description: The terrorist group Hamas released four more hostages from Gaza on Saturday under a ceasefire deal with Israel that took effect nearly a week ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas announced Friday that it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South African president signs controversial land seizure bill, eroding private property rights
item.description: Opponents of a controversial South African land expropriation bill are calling the legislation a threat to private ownership after it was endorsed by the country's president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the escalating conflict in eastern Congo as rebels close in on Goma
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Fighting has escalated sharply in recent weeks in eastern Congo, where rebels have seized key towns and are closing in on the city of Goma, the government’s last stronghold in the region bordering Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel’s military will remain in Lebanon as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Israel said its forces will remain in southern Lebanon after UN and Lebanese forces moved too slowly under the truce with Hezbollah established in November, which remains unfulfilled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Survivor of Nova music festival Hamas terror attack wins slot to represent Israel at Eurovision
item.description: Yuval Raphael, survivor of the Nova music festival terrorist attack by Hamas in October 2023, has turned to music to help her process her trauma and will represent Israel in the Eurovision contest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 12 train passengers killed in India after jumping onto tracks to flee alleged fire
item.description: In India, at least 12 train passengers died and at least six others were injured when they jumped out of their train cars to escape a rumored fire and were struck by another train.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin reportedly concerned over Russia's economy ahead of possible Trump tariffs
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is reportedly worried about the state of the country's economy amid threat of tariffs from the U.S. as President Donald Trump returns to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation
